steps.title: Track host dependence
steps.content: Three-host parasites depended on at least two vertebrate species and declined as sea temperatures rose. Two-host parasites depended on only one vertebrate species and were largely stable.

steps.title: Draw the warming inference
steps.content: A reverses which parasite type was more affected, C denies likely warming effects on the fish, and D states the host-number relationship backward. The pattern suggests warming-related vertebrate changes suppressed some parasites but did not affect two-host parasite abundance. The answer is B.
